post_context.copy:
This bathroom renovation had us excited from day one.

We started with a cramped, outdated layout that wasn't doing this beautiful arched window any justice. Our crew opened the whole space up — repositioned the plumbing to centre a freestanding soaking tub right beneath that arch, where the natural light just pours in. We installed a floating wood vanity with a quartz countertop and black matte fixtures that give the room some edge without competing with the architecture. The large-format floor tile we laid down keeps everything feeling clean and spacious, and here's a detail most people don't think about — with spring moisture creeping in through Toronto's freeze-thaw cycle, we made sure the waterproofing membrane behind those walls was done properly before a single tile went up. That's the stuff you'll never see but will thank yourself for in five years. 🛁

That chandelier hanging above the tub? Yeah, that was the finishing touch that made the whole room click ✨

Our team genuinely loved bringing this one together — it's one of those GTA bathroom projects where every choice just worked.

Thinking about giving your bathroom a fresh start this spring? Send us a message — we'd love to hear what you have in mind 😊

PDX Remodelling Solutions - 437 238 8992

media_context.content_context: This stunning bathroom renovation features a freestanding white soaking tub positioned beneath a large arched window with a chandelier, creating an elegant focal point. The space includes a modern floating wood vanity with a marble or quartz countertop, black matte fixtures, and a large rectangular mirror with industrial-style vanity lighting above. The walls are painted in a soft, warm neutral tone, and the floors appear to be large-format light-colored tile, giving the room a clean and spacious feel. The overall design blends contemporary minimalism with classic architectural details like the arched window.

post_context.copy:
Bathroom tip: Check your caulking before spring moisture sets in.

Right now with snowmelt and all this rain, water is looking for every tiny gap it can find. Run your finger along the caulk lines around your tub and shower — if it feels hard, cracked, or pulls away from the surface even slightly, it's already failing. Here's what most people don't realize: water that gets behind the wall in spring sits there through our humid Toronto summer, and by next winter's freeze-thaw cycles it's expanding inside your wall cavity and causing damage you won't see until it's a serious problem. Fresh silicone caulk is a $12 tube and an hour of your weekend. Strip the old stuff completely with a razor and caulk remover — don't just layer new over old, it won't bond properly. 🛠️

If you're pulling caulk away and finding soft or discoloured drywall behind it, that's a bigger conversation — we're happy to come take a look.

PDX Remodelling Solutions - 437 238 8992
post_context.post_angle: Urge GTA homeowners to inspect and replace cracked caulking around tubs and showers before freeze-thaw cycles allow moisture to seep behind walls.

post_context.copy:
Your bathroom exhaust fan is probably clogged right now.

Spring is the perfect time to pop that cover off and clean it before Toronto's humid summer hits. Pull the grille down, vacuum the dust and lint off the blades — you'll be shocked how packed it gets. Then do the tissue test: turn the fan on and hold a single sheet of toilet paper up to it. If it doesn't stick, your fan is either dying or your duct is partially blocked. A weak fan during July and August in the GTA means moisture just sits on your ceiling and walls, and that's exactly how you get mould behind the paint and in corners you can't even see. Most fans last about 10 years before the motor gets sluggish, and a lot of Toronto homes from the early 2000s are overdue. 🛠️

If you're finding moisture damage that goes deeper than a fan swap, we're happy to come take a look.

PDX Remodelling Solutions - 437 238 8992

post_context.copy:
Save this before summer hits 🔖

Here's what we tell every client who's worried about mould hiding behind their tiles:

1. Run your bathroom fan 20+ minutes after every shower — not just during.
2. Check your fan's CFM rating — most GTA bathrooms need at least 80 CFM.
3. Crack a window on dry spring days to flush trapped moisture now.
4. Inspect grout lines and caulk for cracks — that's where water sneaks behind tiles.
5. If tiles sound hollow when you tap them, moisture may already be trapped back there.
6. Spring is your inspection window — fix waterproofing before humid July air makes it worse.

GTA summers push bathroom humidity through the roof. A little prevention now saves thousands later.

When it goes beyond a checklist fix, we handle it right.
